text: Sleep spindles — brief bursts of oscillatory neural activity at 12-15 Hz — are among the most reliable markers of cognitive recovery. Generated by the thalamus during stage 2 sleep, they act as sensory gates, blocking external noise from disturbing consolidation. Research by Sara Mednick at UC Irvine showed that spindle density in afternoon naps predicts how well declarative memories are protected. More strikingly, people with high spindle counts show less attentional deterioration across a full day of work. The spindle is not a side-effect of sleep — it is an active guardian of focused thought.
question: According to the passage, what does research by Sara Mednick link spindle density to?

options.text: The duration of slow-wave sleep in a full night's rest
How well declarative memories are protected in afternoon naps
The rate at which amyloid-beta proteins are cleared overnight
Improved thalamic regulation of the prefrontal cortex
